items.front: How long must IFR traffic be restricted or suspended for an overdue/unreported aircraft under 10-4-1?
items.back: 30 minutes following the applicable time (approach clearance, EFC, NAVAID arrival, later of facility/pilot estimate at en route fix and destination NAVAID, or release/void time). Unless radar separation is used.

items.front: For how long must airport lighting be operated for an unreported aircraft under 10-4-2?
items.back: At least 30 minutes before ETA until the aircraft is located OR for 30 minutes after fuel supply is estimated exhausted. EN ROUTE requests airport management; TERMINAL operates lights directly.

items.front: When may normal ATC resume after the 30-minute traffic suspension period under 10-4-3?
items.back: If the operators or pilots of other aircraft concur. This concurrence must be maintained for 30 minutes after the suspension period expires.

items.front: What transponder requests can be used to re-establish communication with a NORDO aircraft under 10-4-4c?
items.back: 1. Reply Mode 3/A IDENT. 2. Reply on Code 7600. 3. Change to stand-by (to confirm lack of target is from the requested action).

items.front: After how many minutes of lost communication should a controller consider the activity suspicious under 10-4-4e?
items.back: 5 minutes. Then report to the OS/CIC per FAA Order JO 7610.4, Chapter 7, and paragraph 2-1-26f.

items.front: What are the contact numbers for New York Radio and San Francisco Radio under 10-4-4?
items.back: San Francisco Radio: (800)-621-0140. New York Radio: (800)-645-1095. Used for ACARS relay to reestablish communications.

items.front: What clearance should be broadcast for a lost-comm IFR aircraft to proceed under 10-4-4d?
items.back: A clearance to proceed to its filed alternate airport at the MEA, if the aircraft operator concurs.
